{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Development Officer",
  "description": "Lovonus Microfinance Bank is licensed by the Central Bank of Nigeria under the Bank &amp; Other Financial Institution Act (BOFIA) of 1991 (as amended) and the National Microfinance Regulatory and Supervisory Framework of December 2009

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Business Development Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lekki &amp; Sangotedo, Lagos
Sector: Microfinance Banking

Job Summary


	We are looking for a results-driven and customer-oriented Business Development Officer to join our team.
	In this role, you will be responsible for driving the bank&#39;s growth by identifying new business opportunities, mobilizing deposits, marketing loan products, and managing client relationships.
	The ideal candidate possesses excellent communication skills, a strong network, and a passion for financial inclusion and sales.


Key Responsibilities


	Client Acquisition &amp; Marketing: Source for new individual and corporate clients to actively market the bank&rsquo;s deposit and loan products.
	Deposit Mobilization: Meet and exceed set monthly targets for deposit mobilization and account opening.
	Loan Portfolio Management: Evaluate loan applications, conduct thorough credit appraisals/site visits, and ensure high-quality loan disbursement.
	Relationship Management: Maintain excellent relationships with existing clients to ensure customer retention, repeat business, and referrals.
	Recovery &amp; Monitoring: Monitor loan repayment schedules and follow up with clients to ensure zero tolerance for default and maintain a healthy Portfolio at Risk (PAR).
	Market Intelligence: Keep track of market trends, competitor activities, and customer feedback to suggest product improvements.


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	Education: B.Sc. or HND in Marketing, Business Administration, Banking and Finance, or any related field.
	Experience: 2&ndash;4 years of active experience in marketing or business development within a Microfinance Bank or commercial bank.
	Proven track record of meeting and exceeding sales/mobilization targets.


Skills:


	Strong marketing, negotiation, and persuasion skills.
	Good understanding of micro-lending and credit risk assessment.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Proficiency in basic computer applications (MS Word, Excel).


Personal Attributes:


	Target-driven, self-motivated, honest, and able to work under pressure with minimal supervision.


Salary
N200,000 - N300,000 / month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to:&nbsp;hr@lovonusmfb.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the&nbsp;subject of the&nbsp;email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-04-18",

    "@type": "Organization",

  },

Business Development Officer Job at Lovonus Microfinance Bank




        
    

        
        Lovonus Microfinance Bank is licensed by the Central Bank of Nigeria under the Bank & Other Financial Institution Act (BOFIA) of 1991 (as amended) and the National Microfinance Regulatory and Supervisory Framework of December 2009

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Officer

Job Location: Lekki & Sangotedo, Lagos
Sector: Microfinance Banking

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for a results-driven and customer-oriented Business Development Officer to join our team.
	In this role, you will be responsible for driving the bank's growth by identifying new business opportunities, mobilizing deposits, marketing loan products, and managing client relationships.
	The ideal candidate possesses excellent communication skills, a strong network, and a passion for financial inclusion and sales.


Key Responsibilities


	Client Acquisition & Marketing: Source for new individual and corporate clients to actively market the bank’s deposit and loan products.
	Deposit Mobilization: Meet and exceed set monthly targets for deposit mobilization and account opening.
	Loan Portfolio Management: Evaluate loan applications, conduct thorough credit appraisals/site visits, and ensure high-quality loan disbursement.
	Relationship Management: Maintain excellent relationships with existing clients to ensure customer retention, repeat business, and referrals.
	Recovery & Monitoring: Monitor loan repayment schedules and follow up with clients to ensure zero tolerance for default and maintain a healthy Portfolio at Risk (PAR).
	Market Intelligence: Keep track of market trends, competitor activities, and customer feedback to suggest product improvements.


Requirements & Qualifications


	Education: B.Sc. or HND in Marketing, Business Administration, Banking and Finance, or any related field.
	Experience: 2–4 years of active experience in marketing or business development within a Microfinance Bank or commercial bank.
	Proven track record of meeting and exceeding sales/mobilization targets.


Skills:


	Strong marketing, negotiation, and persuasion skills.
	Good understanding of micro-lending and credit risk assessment.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Proficiency in basic computer applications (MS Word, Excel).


Personal Attributes:


	Target-driven, self-motivated, honest, and able to work under pressure with minimal supervision.


Salary
N200,000 - N300,000 / month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Human Resource Officer",
  "description": "Little Sprouts Schools, Abuja is a globally recognized school that produces future leaders, critical thinkers and disciplined

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	Develop and implement HR strategies, policies, and procedures aligned with the school&rsquo;s vision.
	Oversee staff recruitment, onboarding, contracts, leave management, and performance appraisals.
	Ensure compliance with labor laws, safeguarding, and child protection policies.
	Drive employee engagement, welfare, and professional development initiatives.
	Oversee general administrative functions (correspondence, record-keeping, archiving).
	Supervise facilities management to ensure health, safety, and conducive learning environment.
	Lead staff appraisal cycles for administrative staff and support HoS in appraising teaching staff.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to:&nbsp;hr@lsisabuja.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Human Resource Officer Job at Little Sprouts International School




        
    

        
        Little Sprouts Schools, Abuja is a globally recognized school that produces future leaders, critical thinkers and disciplined

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Officer

Job Location: Abuja
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Develop and implement HR strategies, policies, and procedures aligned with the school’s vision.
	Oversee staff recruitment, onboarding, contracts, leave management, and performance appraisals.
	Ensure compliance with labor laws, safeguarding, and child protection policies.
	Drive employee engagement, welfare, and professional development initiatives.
	Oversee general administrative functions (correspondence, record-keeping, archiving).
	Supervise facilities management to ensure health, safety, and conducive learning environment.
	Lead staff appraisal cycles for administrative staff and support HoS in appraising teaching staff.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Credit Analyst",
  "description": "Lovonus Microfinance Bank is licensed by the Central Bank of Nigeria under the Bank &amp; Other Financial Institution Act (BOFIA) of 1991 (as amended) and the National Microfinance Regulatory and Supervisory Framework of December 2009

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Credit Analyst

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos

Job Summary


	We are seeking an analytical and detail-oriented Credit Officer to join our credit risk management team.
	You will be responsible for evaluating loan applications, assessing the creditworthiness of diverse clients, and managing a healthy loan portfolio.
	Your primary goal is to facilitate business growth while minimizing financial risk through sound lending decisions and proactive monitoring.


Key Responsibilities


	Credit Appraisal: Conduct thorough financial analysis of loan applicants, including cash flow analysis, debt-to-income ratios, and collateral valuation.
	Risk Assessment: Evaluate the credit risk of individuals and businesses based on credit history, financial statements, and industry trends.
	Documentation &amp; Compliance: Ensure all loan files are complete and comply with internal credit policies and Central Bank (CBN) regulatory requirements.
	Portfolio Monitoring: Perform regular site visits and monitor repayment schedules to ensure timely collections and early detection of potential defaults.
	Reporting: Prepare detailed credit memos and monthly reports on portfolio performance, including Non-Performing Loans (NPL) ratios.
	Client Relationship Management: Guide clients through the application process and provide professional advice on appropriate credit facilities.


Required Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Education: Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Banking &amp; Finance, Economics, Accounting, or a related discipline.
	Experience: 3&ndash;5 years of experience in credit operations within a Microfinance Bank or Commercial Bank.


Technical Proficiency:


	Strong understanding of the 5 C&#39;s of Credit (Character, Capacity, Capital, Collateral, and Conditions).
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel for financial modeling.
	Experience with Credit Bureau reporting and inquiries.
	Soft Skills: Exceptional integrity, strong negotiation skills, and the ability to make objective, data-driven decisions.


Competencies:


	Deep knowledge of local lending laws and debt recovery procedures.
	Ability to interpret complex financial statements and tax returns.
	High level of accuracy and attention to detail.


Salary
N200,000 - N300,000 / month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to:&nbsp;hr@lovonusmfb.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the&nbsp;subject of the&nbsp;email.",

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Credit Analyst

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking an analytical and detail-oriented Credit Officer to join our credit risk management team.
	You will be responsible for evaluating loan applications, assessing the creditworthiness of diverse clients, and managing a healthy loan portfolio.
	Your primary goal is to facilitate business growth while minimizing financial risk through sound lending decisions and proactive monitoring.


Key Responsibilities


	Credit Appraisal: Conduct thorough financial analysis of loan applicants, including cash flow analysis, debt-to-income ratios, and collateral valuation.
	Risk Assessment: Evaluate the credit risk of individuals and businesses based on credit history, financial statements, and industry trends.
	Documentation & Compliance: Ensure all loan files are complete and comply with internal credit policies and Central Bank (CBN) regulatory requirements.
	Portfolio Monitoring: Perform regular site visits and monitor repayment schedules to ensure timely collections and early detection of potential defaults.
	Reporting: Prepare detailed credit memos and monthly reports on portfolio performance, including Non-Performing Loans (NPL) ratios.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Head, Internal Control & Audit",
  "description": "Lovonus Microfinance Bank is licensed by the Central Bank of Nigeria under the Bank &amp; Other Financial Institution Act (BOFIA) of 1991 (as amended) and the National Microfinance Regulatory and Supervisory Framework of December 2009

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head, Internal Control &amp; Audit

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Reports To: Managing Director / Board Audit Committee

Job&nbsp;Description


	We are seeking a seasoned, highly analytical, and ethical professional to lead our Internal Control and Audit department.
	The Head of Internal Control &amp; Audit will be responsible for developing and executing a comprehensive internal audit program, ensuring strict regulatory compliance, safeguarding bank assets, and improving the overall operational efficiency of the institution.
	The ideal candidate will have a deep understanding of CBN regulations, risk management, and microfinance banking operations, coupled with an uncompromising stance on integrity.


Key Responsibilities


	Audit Planning &amp; Execution: Formulate, implement, and manage the annual risk-based internal audit plan to assess the adequacy and effectiveness of the bank&rsquo;s internal controls.
	Regulatory Compliance: Ensure full compliance with Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) regulations, NDIC guidelines, and other relevant statutory requirements.
	Risk Assessment: Identify, assess, and evaluate operational, financial, and credit risks across all branches and departments.
	Investigation &amp; Fraud Prevention: Lead investigations into suspected fraud, forgeries, or operational lapses and recommend appropriate disciplinary or corrective actions.
	Financial &amp; Operational Reviews: Review accuracy and reliability of financial reports, loan disbursements, and general ledger reconciliations.
	Reporting: Prepare and present independent, objective, and timely audit reports to the Managing Director and the Board Audit Committee.
	Internal Control Maintenance: Continuously review and update the bank&rsquo;s internal control policies to plug loopholes and adapt to evolving security or operational needs.


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	Education: Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or a related discipline.
	Professional Certification: Must be a Chartered Accountant (ICAN or ACCA). Certifications such as CISA, CIA, or CFE are a distinct advantage.
	Experience: Minimum of 5&ndash;10 years of experience in internal control, audit, or risk management within the banking sector.
	At least 3 years in a senior or managerial role (Microfinance experience is highly preferred).
	Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to hr@lovonusmfb.com using &quot;Head, Internal Control &amp; Audit&quot; as the subject of the mail.


Salary
N400,000 - N500,000 / month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to:&nbsp;hr@lovonusmfb.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the&nbsp;subject of the&nbsp;email.",

Head, Internal Control & Audit Job at Lovonus Microfinance Bank




        
    

        
        Lovonus Microfinance Bank is licensed by the Central Bank of Nigeria under the Bank & Other Financial Institution Act (BOFIA) of 1991 (as amended) and the National Microfinance Regulatory and Supervisory Framework of December 2009

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head, Internal Control & Audit

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Reports To: Managing Director / Board Audit Committee

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a seasoned, highly analytical, and ethical professional to lead our Internal Control and Audit department.
	The Head of Internal Control & Audit will be responsible for developing and executing a comprehensive internal audit program, ensuring strict regulatory compliance, safeguarding bank assets, and improving the overall operational efficiency of the institution.
	The ideal candidate will have a deep understanding of CBN regulations, risk management, and microfinance banking operations, coupled with an uncompromising stance on integrity.


Key Responsibilities


	Audit Planning & Execution: Formulate, implement, and manage the annual risk-based internal audit plan to assess the adequacy and effectiveness of the bank’s internal controls.
	Regulatory Compliance: Ensure full compliance with Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) regulations, NDIC guidelines, and other relevant statutory requirements.
	Risk Assessment: Identify, assess, and evaluate operational, financial, and credit risks across all branches and departments.
	Investigation & Fraud Prevention: Lead investigations into suspected fraud, forgeries, or operational lapses and recommend appropriate disciplinary or corrective actions.
	Financial & Operational Reviews: Review accuracy and reliability of financial reports, loan disbursements, and general ledger reconciliations.
	Reporting: Prepare and present independent, objective, and timely audit reports to the Managing Director and the Board Audit Committee.
	Internal Control Maintenance: Continuously review and update the bank’s internal control policies to plug loopholes and adapt to evolving security or operational needs.


Requirements & Qualifications


	Education: Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or a related discipline.
	Professional Certification: Must be a Chartered Accountant (ICAN or ACCA). Certifications such as CISA, CIA, or CFE are a distinct advantage.
	Experience: Minimum of 5–10 years of experience in internal control, audit, or risk management within the banking sector.
	At least 3 years in a senior or managerial role (Microfinance experience is highly preferred).
	Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to [email protected] using "Head, Internal Control & Audit" as the subject of the mail.


Salary
N400,000 - N500,000 / month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Sales Executive",
  "description": "Montaigne Place is a leading luxury retail company that offers an exquisite range of cosmetics, fragrances, skincare, lifestyle products, spa and wellness services. Established in 1995, Montaigne Place is a trading name of Montaigne AH. Ltd. began its journey in the Nigerian beauty sector as exclusive agents and distributors of the prestigious Clarins brand.

Today, Montaigne Place operations extend across retail, wholesale, spa &amp; wellness, with its flagship retail division, Montaigne Place, boasting a network of Forty (40) outlets throughout Nigeria. These outlets are strategically located in Lagos, the country&#39;s commercial hub, as well as in Abuja, the capital city, and Port Harcourt, along with other prominent cities.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Executive

Job Location:&nbsp;Victoria Island / GRA - Lagos

Job&nbsp;Description


	Acts as advisor or consultants to walk-in customers and over-the-phone customers.
	Provide friendly, efficient service to all walk-in customers.
	Offer information about merchandise selection to help customers choose wisely.
	Have deep product knowledge and exhibit familiarity with all product information.
	Actively listen to customers and then provide them with solutions that are exactly what they need.

Sales Executive Job at Montaigne Place




        
    

        
        Montaigne Place is a leading luxury retail company that offers an exquisite range of cosmetics, fragrances, skincare, lifestyle products, spa and wellness services. Established in 1995, Montaigne Place is a trading name of Montaigne AH. Ltd. began its journey in the Nigerian beauty sector as exclusive agents and distributors of the prestigious Clarins brand.

Today, Montaigne Place operations extend across retail, wholesale, spa & wellness, with its flagship retail division, Montaigne Place, boasting a network of Forty (40) outlets throughout Nigeria. These outlets are strategically located in Lagos, the country's commercial hub, as well as in Abuja, the capital city, and Port Harcourt, along with other prominent cities.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Executive

Job Location: Victoria Island / GRA - Lagos

Job Description


	Acts as advisor or consultants to walk-in customers and over-the-phone customers.
	Provide friendly, efficient service to all walk-in customers.
	Offer information about merchandise selection to help customers choose wisely.
	Have deep product knowledge and exhibit familiarity with all product information.
	Actively listen to customers and then provide them with solutions that are exactly what they need.
	Excite customers about how new products can enhance their lives.
	Assist customer by suggesting additional merchandise that compliments their selections.
	Assist in pricing merchandise, markdowns, restocking and other retail related duties.
	Be able to effectively handle customer service issues.
	Assist and maintain visual merchandising and selling floor standards.
	Know and use promotional event information to maximize sales.
	Assist in preparation of periodic physical inventory exercises.
	Be able to use PoS Systems for sales & other check-out functions.
	Support Outlet Manager to execute the display and maintenance of off-shelf merchandise.


Salary
N150,000 – N160,000 Monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as subject of the mail.
